naturalLanguage: An Appalachian mountain dulcimer is a musical instrument: a fretted string instrument played by plucking or strumming, whose construction and tuning are specific to the Appalachian mountain tradition.

A banjo is a musical instrument with a circular membrane head and 4-5 strings, derived from West African lute instruments (kora, ngoni) and developed into its current form through African American communities in the American South.

A frame drum is a musical instrument: a single-headed drum in which the playing head is attached to a shallow circular hoop frame, used across Anishinaabe, Cree, and many other First Nations musical traditions.

Frame drum construction is a heritage skill: the process of selecting and preparing the hoop, soaking and stretching the hide membrane, and lashing the membrane to the hoop — knowledge transmitted within First Nations communities across generations.

Katajjaq (Inuit throat singing) is a form of singing: a vocal performance practice in which two singers face each other and produce interlocking rhythmic breath patterns, continuing until one singer laughs or loses the rhythm.

A qilaut (Inuit frame drum) is a musical instrument: a large single-headed frame drum held in one hand and struck on the inside edge of the rim with a short stick, used in Inuit drum dance traditions.

Shape-note singing (Sacred Harp) is a musical tradition: a community vocal practice using a notation system where different note shapes indicate solfege syllables, enabling community members without formal music training to read and sing four-part harmony.

Mountain dulcimer making is an Appalachian heritage skill: constructing the fretted hourglass-shaped body, installing the tuning pegs, placing the frets, and stringing the instrument — knowledge transmitted in the Appalachian mountain tradition.

A traditional wooden flute (as made in many First Nations traditions) is a musical instrument: a tubular wind instrument with finger holes cut to produce a scale, constructed from a hollowed branch or drilled cylinder of wood.
